title: The Four Pillars of UX Measurement
description: A visual framework categorizing the key quantitative and qualitative measures used to evaluate user experience.
elements: Efficiency (How easily can they do it?)
Satisfaction (How do they feel about it?)
Error Tolerance (How do they recover from mistakes?)
relationships: These four core measures are interconnected and provide a holistic view of a product's user experience. They are often tracked through a combination of performance metrics and attitudinal surveys.
